question: Zinc reacts with hydrochloric acid: Zn(s) + 2HCl(aq) â†’ ZnClâ‚‚(aq) + Hâ‚‚(g). If 0.10 mol of Zn is added to 0.15 mol of HCl, which reactant is limiting?
explanation: From the equation, 1 mol Zn needs 2 mol HCl. 0.10 mol Zn would need 0.20 mol HCl, but only 0.15 mol is available. HCl is used up first, so it is limiting.

question: Hydrogen and oxygen react: 2Hâ‚‚(g) + Oâ‚‚(g) â†’ 2Hâ‚‚O(l). If 48 dmÂ³ of Hâ‚‚ reacts with 12 dmÂ³ of Oâ‚‚ at r.t.p., what is the total volume of gas remaining after the reaction? [1 mol gas = 24 dmÂ³ at r.t.p.; Hâ‚‚O is liquid at r.t.p.]
explanation: Volume ratio Hâ‚‚:Oâ‚‚ = 2:1. 12 dmÂ³ Oâ‚‚ needs 24 dmÂ³ Hâ‚‚. Hâ‚‚ used = 24 dmÂ³, excess Hâ‚‚ = 48 - 24 = 24 dmÂ³. Hâ‚‚O is liquid, so only excess Hâ‚‚ remains as gas. Total gas = 24 dmÂ³.

question: 25.0 cmÂ³ of 0.20 mol/dmÂ³ NaOH reacts with 20.0 cmÂ³ of 0.15 mol/dmÂ³ HCl: NaOH + HCl â†’ NaCl + Hâ‚‚O. Which reactant is limiting?
explanation: Moles NaOH = 0.20 Ã— 0.025 = 0.0050 mol. Moles HCl = 0.15 Ã— 0.020 = 0.0030 mol. Ratio is 1:1, so HCl (0.0030 mol) is limiting as less is available.

question: Methane burns: CHâ‚„(g) + 2Oâ‚‚(g) â†’ COâ‚‚(g) + 2Hâ‚‚O(l). If 12 dmÂ³ of CHâ‚„ reacts with 40 dmÂ³ of Oâ‚‚ at r.t.p., what is the total volume of gas after the reaction? [Hâ‚‚O is liquid at r.t.p.]
explanation: Volume ratio CHâ‚„:Oâ‚‚ = 1:2. 12 dmÂ³ CHâ‚„ needs 24 dmÂ³ Oâ‚‚. Oâ‚‚ is in excess by 40 - 24 = 16 dmÂ³. COâ‚‚ produced = 12 dmÂ³ (1:1 with CHâ‚„). Total gas = excess Oâ‚‚ (16 dmÂ³) + COâ‚‚ (12 dmÂ³) = 28 dmÂ³.

question: Magnesium reacts with sulfuric acid: Mg(s) + Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„(aq) â†’ MgSOâ‚„(aq) + Hâ‚‚(g). If 0.05 mol Mg reacts with 0.03 mol Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„, what is the maximum moles of Hâ‚‚ produced?
explanation: Ratio Mg:Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„:Hâ‚‚ = 1:1:1. Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„ (0.03 mol) is limiting. Maximum Hâ‚‚ = moles of limiting reactant = 0.03 mol.

question: Nitrogen and hydrogen react: Nâ‚‚(g) + 3Hâ‚‚(g) â†’ 2NHâ‚ƒ(g). If 28 g of Nâ‚‚ reacts with 6.0 g of Hâ‚‚, what is the total volume of gas after the reaction at r.t.p.? [Ar: N=14, H=1; 1 mol = 24 dmÂ³]
explanation: Moles Nâ‚‚ = 28/28 = 1.0 mol. Moles Hâ‚‚ = 6.0/2 = 3.0 mol. Ratio Nâ‚‚:Hâ‚‚ = 1:3, so exact stoichiometric amounts. Both fully react. NHâ‚ƒ produced = 2.0 mol. Volume = 2.0 Ã— 24 = 48 dmÂ³. No excess gas remains.

question: Ethene burns: Câ‚‚Hâ‚„(g) + 3Oâ‚‚(g) â†’ 2COâ‚‚(g) + 2Hâ‚‚O(l). If 10 dmÂ³ of Câ‚‚Hâ‚„ reacts with 50 dmÂ³ of Oâ‚‚ at r.t.p., what is the total volume of gas after reaction? [Hâ‚‚O is liquid]
explanation: Volume ratio Câ‚‚Hâ‚„:Oâ‚‚ = 1:3. 10 dmÂ³ Câ‚‚Hâ‚„ needs 30 dmÂ³ Oâ‚‚. Excess Oâ‚‚ = 50 - 30 = 20 dmÂ³. COâ‚‚ produced = 2 Ã— 10 = 20 dmÂ³ (ratio 1:2). Total gas = 20 + 20 = 40 dmÂ³.

question: 50.0 cmÂ³ of 0.40 mol/dmÂ³ HCl reacts with excess magnesium: Mg + 2HCl â†’ MgClâ‚‚ + Hâ‚‚. What volume of Hâ‚‚ is produced at r.t.p.? [1 mol gas = 24 dmÂ³]
explanation: Moles HCl = 0.40 Ã— 0.050 = 0.020 mol. Ratio 2HCl:1Hâ‚‚, so moles Hâ‚‚ = 0.020/2 = 0.010 mol. Volume = 0.010 Ã— 24 = 0.24 dmÂ³.

question: Carbon monoxide burns: 2CO(g) + Oâ‚‚(g) â†’ 2COâ‚‚(g). If 30 dmÂ³ of CO reacts with 20 dmÂ³ of Oâ‚‚ at r.t.p., what is the total volume of gas after reaction?
explanation: Volume ratio CO:Oâ‚‚ = 2:1. 30 dmÂ³ CO needs 15 dmÂ³ Oâ‚‚. Excess Oâ‚‚ = 20 - 15 = 5 dmÂ³. COâ‚‚ produced = 30 dmÂ³ (ratio CO:COâ‚‚ = 1:1). Total gas = excess Oâ‚‚ (5 dmÂ³) + COâ‚‚ (30 dmÂ³) = 35 dmÂ³.

question: In a titration, 20.0 cmÂ³ of Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„ reacts with 25.0 cmÂ³ of 0.20 mol/dmÂ³ NaOH: 2NaOH + Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„ â†’ Naâ‚‚SOâ‚„ + 2Hâ‚‚O. What is the concentration of Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„?
explanation: Moles NaOH = 0.20 Ã— 0.025 = 0.0050 mol. Ratio NaOH:Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„ = 2:1, so moles Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„ = 0.0050/2 = 0.0025 mol. Concentration = 0.0025/0.020 = 0.125 mol/dmÂ³.

question: Ammonia synthesis: Nâ‚‚(g) + 3Hâ‚‚(g) â†’ 2NHâ‚ƒ(g). If 1.0 mol Nâ‚‚ is mixed with 2.0 mol Hâ‚‚, what is the total moles of gas after the reaction is complete?
explanation: Ratio Nâ‚‚:Hâ‚‚ = 1:3. 1.0 mol Nâ‚‚ needs 3.0 mol Hâ‚‚, but only 2.0 mol Hâ‚‚ available, so Hâ‚‚ is limiting. 2.0 mol Hâ‚‚ reacts with 2/3 = 0.67 mol Nâ‚‚, producing 4/3 = 1.33 mol NHâ‚ƒ. Excess Nâ‚‚ = 1.0 - 0.67 = 0.33 mol. Total gas = excess Nâ‚‚ (0.33) + NHâ‚ƒ (1.33) = 1.67 mol.

question: A metal M reacts with hydrochloric acid: M(s) + 2HCl(aq) â†’ MClâ‚‚(aq) + Hâ‚‚(g). 2.4 g of M reacts with excess HCl to produce 2.4 dmÂ³ of Hâ‚‚ at r.t.p. What is the Aáµ£ of M? [1 mol gas = 24 dmÂ³]
explanation: Moles Hâ‚‚ = 2.4/24 = 0.10 mol. Ratio M:Hâ‚‚ = 1:1, so moles M = 0.10 mol. Aáµ£ = mass/moles = 2.4/0.10 = 24. Metal M is magnesium.

question: Propane burns: Câ‚ƒHâ‚ˆ(g) + 5Oâ‚‚(g) â†’ 3COâ‚‚(g) + 4Hâ‚‚O(l). If 20 dmÂ³ of Câ‚ƒH reacts with 120 dmÂ³ of Oâ‚‚ at r.t.p., what is the total volume of gas after reaction? [Hâ‚‚O is liquid]
explanation: Volume ratio Câ‚ƒH:Oâ‚‚ = 1:5. 20 dmÂ³ Câ‚ƒHâ‚ˆ needs 100 dmÂ³ Oâ‚‚. Excess Oâ‚‚ = 120 - 100 = 20 dmÂ³. COâ‚‚ produced = 3 Ã— 20 = 60 dmÂ³ (ratio 1:3). Total gas = excess Oâ‚‚ (20) + COâ‚‚ (60) = 80 dmÂ³.

question: Iron reacts with copper(II) sulfate: Fe(s) + CuSOâ‚„(aq) â†’ FeSOâ‚„(aq) + Cu(s). If 5.6 g Fe is added to 100 cmÂ³ of 0.50 mol/dmÂ³ CuSOâ‚„, what mass of Cu is produced? [Ar: Fe=56, Cu=64]
explanation: Moles Fe = 5.6/56 = 0.10 mol. Moles CuSOâ‚„ = 0.50 Ã— 0.100 = 0.050 mol. Ratio 1:1, so CuSOâ‚„ is limiting. Moles Cu = 0.050 mol. Mass Cu = 0.050 Ã— 64 = 3.2 g.

question: Hydrogen sulfide burns: 2Hâ‚‚S(g) + 3Oâ‚‚(g) â†’ 2SOâ‚‚(g) + 2Hâ‚‚O(l). If 24 dmÂ³ of Hâ‚‚S reacts with 48 dmÂ³ of Oâ‚‚ at r.t.p., what is the total volume of gas after reaction? [Hâ‚‚O is liquid]
explanation: Volume ratio Hâ‚‚S:Oâ‚‚ = 2:3. 24 dmÂ³ Hâ‚‚S needs (3/2)Ã—24 = 36 dmÂ³ Oâ‚‚. Excess Oâ‚‚ = 48 - 36 = 12 dmÂ³. SOâ‚‚ produced = 24 dmÂ³ (ratio Hâ‚‚S:SOâ‚‚ = 1:1). Total gas = excess Oâ‚‚ (12) + SOâ‚‚ (24) = 36 dmÂ³.
